edges.ev: ZmCLE7 signal passing through CT2 via FEA2 receptor
FEA2 also participates in ZmFCP1 signaling
Both fea3 and fea2 mutants are resistant to the ZmFCP1 peptide
FEA2 interacted with CT2 in co-immunoprecipitation experiments
ZmCRN-mCherry was able to pull down FEA2-Myc
ct2 mutants had larger SAMs
Zmcrn mutants had larger vegetative shoot meristems
fea2 vegetative meristems were larger than normal
fea3 mutant background only partially rescued meristem size when ZmFCP1 expressed
fea4 mutants were also characterized by a larger than normal SAM diameter
FEA4 bound the promoter of auxin response factor genes; ARF6 downregulated in fea4 mutants
FEA4 bound to the promoter of IAA24
ARF8 downregulated (P=0.002) in fea4 mutants
BARREN STALK1 downregulated (P=0.002) in fea4 mutants
RAMOSA3 downregulated (P=1.79e-15) in fea4 mutants
FEA4 binds and activates RA1 which imposes determinacy
GNARLEY1 is positively modulated by FEA4 and repressed by KN1
GNARLEY1 is positively modulated by FEA4 and repressed by KN1
FEA4 bound a maize ortholog of AG, ZAG1 (binding in third intron)
Larger SAM correlates with elevated kernel row number (17.14 WT vs 28 fea4 in B73)
FEA4 activity is negatively regulated by MSCA1; msca1-ref fea4 double = fea4 single
Abphyl2 dominant mutants show enlarged meristem nearly 2-fold wild-type
ABPH1 is a cytokinin-inducible type-A response regulator
abph1 mutants have enlarged shoot apical meristem
ABPH1 acts both as a positive regulator of auxin levels and PIN1 signaling
Treatment with polar auxin transport inhibitor strongly reduced ABPH1 expression
The phytohormone cytokinin induces WUS expression
type-B ARRs directly bind to the WUS proximal promoter region and activate its expression
Cytokinin signaling activates type-B ARRs which then regulate ZmWUS1
WUS promotes proliferation of stem cells; Bif3 duplicate drives hyper IM
In the CZ, WUS activates the CLV3 gene
A distinct ZmFCP1-FEA3 ligand-receptor combination prevents WUS gene expression in the rib zone
vt2 tryptophan aminotransferase shows significant up-regulation in Kn1-N leaves
teosinte branched1 harbors a region that behaves as an enhancer with KN1-binding motifs
KN1 binds to nearly half of the AUX-IAA and ARF genes annotated in the maize genome
kn1 loss-of-function plants occasionally lack shoots; null allele kn1-e1 produces a small cob with poor kernel yield

perturbations.ev: fea2 ears can have up to double the normal number of vertical rows of seeds (26-36 vs 16-18)
weak allele of FEA2 increases KRN without the fasciation phenotype of stronger alleles
fea3 mutants show enlarged inflorescence meristems and increased kernel rows
kernel row number: wild-type 17.14 vs fea4 28 (B73); wild-type 15.80 vs fea4 19.67 (A619)
ct2 mutants have enlarged SAMs and fasciated ears
Zmcrn mutants had larger vegetative shoot meristems and developed fasciated ear phenotype
Zmcrn heterozygotes had 0.5 to 1.4 more rows than WT
Promoter edited alleles of ZmCLE7 have larger IM diameters and increased kernel row numbers
Edited lines showed increased kernel row numbers and grain yields
Coding region-edited alleles of ZmCLE1E5 have larger IM diameters
fea2 fea4 double mutants synergistic SAM 347 vs 205 (fea4) or 202 (fea2) um
fea3;fea2 double mutants had the largest meristems
abph1 causes decussate phyllotaxy; KRN effect not reported - unchanged for KRN direction
Abphyl2 causes meristem enlargement but phyllotaxy phenotype; KRN not scored
Bif3 dominant mutation produces ball-shaped ears with highly disorganized meristems
kn1 loss-of-function plants occasionally lack shoots; null allele produces a small cob with poor kernel yield
